utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so bryan it's nice to see you again in the office today what's going on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i was in my yard yesterday and i was raking leaves and i felt fine and then when i got into my house about two hours later my back started tightening up and i started getting pins and needles in my right foot

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright have you ever had this type of back pain before

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i had it once about three years ago but it went away after a day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and did you try anything for the pain yet did you take anything or have you have you tried icing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: put some ice on it and i tried two advils and it did n't help

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay does it get better when you're laying down sitting standing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i think the only thing that helped was a hot shower and it feels a little better when i sit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so you said you were raking leaves and now it's been kind of that weather outside for cleaning out the yard have you been doing a lot of yard work lately

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i i normally do n't do any physical activity

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i hear you i make a i make my husband do all the yard work too so alright let me take a look at that low back if if you bend over does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it hurts a little bit when i bend over yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay how about when you stand back up

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so no pain on extension some pain on flexion how about when i push right here on your back

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so some pain to palpation right around the l5 can i have that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: what happens when family members chime in like i'm doing right now because i actually think it was it hurts more when he stands up because i've watched him bend over but it's when he stands up that it really hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and and what's your relationship

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: partners

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so your partner is here with you today and reports that he is also having pain when you are standing up

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've spent so many times you know who i am

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so that's right you always come in together have you noticed anything else partner when when he is

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm worried about it because he is you know his pain tolerance is so high and he would n't be saying anything if it really was n't a problem because it's not at all like last week when he thought about raking leaves but did n't actually rake leaves but then his back did n't hurt as much but this time it really hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so you think this is more of a long term injury

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no he was just thinking about it hurting when he thought about raking but it did n't

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and have you noticed any any prior pain with with the back

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't want to take this away you better go back to talking to him

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's alright let's see how it turns out okay so how about if you if i push down on both your feet like this can you push back up on my hands

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: can you repeat that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: if i'm pushing down on your feet can you push push your feet back

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so your dorsiflexion is normal looks like pulses are equal in all extremities you said you had a little bit of a tingling sensation in which leg

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that would be my right leg

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so some tingling in your right leg alright so i know that you had an x-ray when you came in because we always do x-rays when we have our patients come in of your low back and everything looks normal from that perspective so for your back pain sounds like you probably sprained your low back so i what i want you to do is let's rest it i'm going to prescribe some meloxicam have you taken that before

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have not taken meloxicam

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so i'll prescribe that i'm also gon na prescribe some physical therapy i know you said you have n't been overly active and your partner reported the same thing for a long time so we might wan na get you into some pt and hopefully get you back back in shape get you some stretches that you can do and some exercises and then you know if it's not improving i want you to send me a message and we can possibly look into see if there is anything else going on potentially an mri if it's still not improving does that sound like an okay plan

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it sounds reasonable

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think he should have an mri now i think he had surgery yesterday i think he should have an mri now this it's been hurting for like six hours now and she had an mri we are really worried about it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright you know let's give it a couple of weeks and let's give it two weeks and then call me back and we'll get you an mri if it's still not improving

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good answer

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright alright hope you feel better

detail_list.summary: The doctor initiates the visit to discuss the patient's low back pain.
The patient is a 39-year-old male presenting with low back pain after raking leaves.
The patient reports low back tightening and tingling in the right foot starting two hours after raking leaves.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced this type of back pain previously.
The patient had a similar episode three years ago that resolved in one day.
The doctor asks about previous treatments or attempts to alleviate the pain.
The patient tried ice and two Advil tablets without relief.
The doctor asks about aggravating or alleviating factors related to position.
The patient reports pain relief from a hot shower and sitting.
The doctor asks about the patient's recent physical activity levels.
The patient reports low levels of physical activity.
The doctor initiates a physical examination of the lumbar spine.
The doctor asks if bending over causes pain.
The patient reports pain with flexion.
The doctor asks if standing back up causes pain.
The patient reports no pain on extension.
The doctor asks if palpation of the back causes pain.
The patient reports pain with palpation.
The doctor identifies pain on palpation around L5.
The patient's partner reports that the patient's pain worsens when standing up.
The doctor asks about the relationship between the patient and the person accompanying him.
The patient and the person accompanying him are partners.
The doctor acknowledges the partner's report of pain upon standing.
The doctor asks the patient to perform a dorsiflexion test.
The doctor confirms normal dorsiflexion and equal pulses in all extremities.
The doctor asks which leg is experiencing tingling.
The patient reports tingling in the right leg.
The doctor reports normal X-ray results and assesses the condition as a low back sprain.
The doctor asks if the patient has previously taken meloxicam.
The patient has not taken meloxicam before.
The doctor recommends rest, meloxicam, and physical therapy, with a potential MRI if symptoms do not improve.
The partner requests an MRI for the patient.
The doctor advises a two-week follow-up period before considering an MRI.
